isolation.surface: command-line static checks over README.md and docs/
constraints: No credentials, .env, config.yaml, or auth material accessed or created

commandsRun: Grep README.md and docs/**/*.md for sponsor/referral/discount/invite-code/promotional terms
Grep README.md and docs/**/*.md for auth-related terms to identify refreshed auth docs
python3 static scan of README.md and docs/**/*.md for promotional terms and CJK/non-English lines

assertions.title: Auth docs are English-only and non-promotional
assertions.reason: Found 12 sponsor/referral/discount/invite-code/promotional term matches in README/docs, including README.md Sponsor section lines 11-37. Found 188 CJK/non-English lines and 5 localized docs under docs/; this violates the English-only requirement.

notableFindings: README.md contains a Sponsor section with PackyCode, AICodeMirror, BmoPlus, VisionCoder, discount, promo code, invitecode, aff=, and top-up language.
docs/ contains Chinese localized Markdown files such as docs/gitlab-duo_CN.md, docs/sdk-access_CN.md, docs/sdk-advanced_CN.md, docs/sdk-usage_CN.md, and docs/sdk-watcher_CN.md.
README.md refreshed auth sections for x.ai OAuth, Z.AI, and OpenCode are factual in the reviewed sample, but the broader README/docs surface fails the non-promotional and English-only checks.

summary: Tested 1 assertion: 0 passed, 1 failed. VAL-DOCS-009 failed because promotional sponsor language and non-English/localized docs remain in README.md/docs.
